chapter_title.en: Chapter 34 – The wine made into vinegar

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/295)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/295)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْخَمْرِ يُصْنَعُ فِيهَا الشَّيْ‏ءُ حَتَّى تُحَمِّضَ قَالَ إِذَا كَانَ الَّذِي صُنِعَ فِيهَا هُوَ الْغَالِبُ عَلَى مَا صُنِعَ فِيهِ فَلَا بَأْسَ بِهِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Ibn Bukeyr, from Abu Baseer who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the wine, something is done in it until it acidifies. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If that which was done in it, it overcame upon what was made in it, so there is no problem with it’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid from ibn Bukayr from abu Basir who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about wine to which something is done until it is turned sour. He (the Imam) said, ’If what is done in it (the wine) overwhelms it, then is not harmful/”
